reasoning: The development and approval of a new vaccine typically takes years, even for late-stage candidates. Currently, the most advanced Norovirus vaccine candidate is Moderna's mRNA-1403, which just entered Phase 3 clinical trials in September 2024. Considering the timeline required for Phase 3 completion, data analysis, and regulatory approval, it is highly unlikely that a vaccine will be approved and subsequently recorded as 'Yes' on the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' by February 2026. Therefore, the probability of the status changing from 'No' to 'Yes' within this timeframe is very low.
